title: Mermaid collaboration server CLI with start/stop/status commands
purpose: Provides command-line interface to manage a Mermaid collaboration server, including starting, stopping, and checking server status with process management and logging.
module_context: Defines constants for data directory, PID file, log file, server script path, UI distribution directory, and default port. Sets up command-line argument parsing and dispatches to appropriate handler functions based on the command provided.

steps.content: Create the data directory if it does not exist.
Ensure the directory is created recursively.
Check if the UI directory exists.
IF the UI directory does not exist, log an error and return false.
Log that the UI build process is starting.
Execute the build command for the UI using Bun.
IF the build command fails, log an error and return false.
Log that the UI build is complete and return true.
Check if the PID file exists.
IF the PID file does not exist, return null.
Read the content of the PID file.
Parse the content as an integer.
IF the parsed value is not a valid number, return null.
Return the parsed PID value.
Attempt to send a signal 0 to the process with the given PID.
IF the signal is successfully sent, return true.
ELSE return false.
Record the start time for the wait period.
Construct the URL to check for server readiness.
Loop while the elapsed time is less than the maximum wait time.
Attempt to fetch the server URL.
IF the fetch succeeds with a successful or 404 status, return true.
ELSE wait for 200 milliseconds before checking again.
IF the maximum wait time is exceeded, return false.
Ensure the data directory exists.
Read the existing PID from the file.
IF a PID exists and the process is running, log that the server is already running.
ELSE check if the server script exists.
IF the server script does not exist, log an error and exit.
Check if the UI distribution directory exists.
IF the UI distribution directory does not exist, attempt to build the UI.
IF the UI build fails, log a warning but continue.
Spawn a detached child process to run the server script.
Pipe the stdout and stderr of the child process to the log file.
Write the PID of the spawned process to the PID file.
Log that the server is starting.
Wait for the server to become ready.
IF the server becomes ready, log the success message and location.
ELSE log an error and exit with failure code.
Read the PID from the file.
IF no PID is found, log that the server is not running.
ELSE check if the process with the PID is running.
IF the process is not running, remove the stale PID file.
ELSE send a SIGTERM signal to the process to initiate graceful shutdown.
Wait for up to 3 seconds for the process to shut down gracefully.
IF the process is still running after the wait period, send a SIGKILL signal to force shutdown.
Remove the PID file after stopping the process.
Log that the server has been stopped.
Read the PID from the file.
IF no PID is found, log that the server is stopped.
ELSE check if the process with the PID is running.
IF the process is running, log that the server is running with PID and URL.
ELSE log that the server is stopped due to a stale PID file.
Remove the stale PID file.
